go_no_go_recommendation: Proceed with Caution. While the project has a solid foundation and clear objectives, immediate attention is required to ensure GDPR compliance and volunteer safety protocols are in place. The outlined actions must be executed promptly to mitigate risks associated with data privacy and volunteer safety, which are critical for the project's success.
combined_summary: The three most critical, immediate actions are to establish the cooling center network, finalize the outreach strategy, and ensure GDPR compliance. These actions are essential as they lay the groundwork for operational readiness, ensuring vulnerable populations have access to cooling resources while maintaining legal compliance regarding data handling. Establishing cooling centers and outreach will directly impact the program's ability to reduce heat-related harm effectively.

feedback.title: Establish Cooling Center Network
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Identify at least 10 existing municipal assets (libraries, community centers, etc.) in Thessaloniki suitable for cooling centers by 2026-02-15. 
- Contract with local service providers for staffing these centers, ensuring at least 2 staff members per center during operational hours, finalized by 2026-02-28. 
- Develop a schedule for cooling center hours, ensuring they are open at least 12 hours a day during heat alerts, to be published by 2026-03-01.

feedback.title: Finalize Outreach Strategy
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Create a list of at least 5 local NGOs and social services to partner with for outreach by 2026-02-10. 
- Develop outreach training materials and conduct training sessions for at least 30 volunteers by 2026-02-20. 
- Establish a phone line for outreach registration and support, operational by 2026-02-25.

feedback.title: Procure Home Intervention Supplies
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Identify and procure at least 500 exterior shading kits and 500 reflective blinds by 2026-03-15. 
- Establish a distribution plan for these supplies, ensuring delivery to high-risk homes by 2026-03-30. 
- Hire at least 5 handypersons for installation support, with contracts finalized by 2026-03-20.

feedback.title: Implement Transport Support Plan
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Contract with at least 2 local taxi services for transport support for mobility-limited residents by 2026-02-28. 
- Develop a volunteer driver program, recruiting at least 10 volunteers by 2026-03-05. 
- Create a transport request system to ensure average response times are under 45 minutes during heat alerts, operational by 2026-03-10.

feedback.title: Ensure GDPR Compliance
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Engage a GDPR compliance expert to conduct a data privacy impact assessment (DPIA) by 2026-02-15. 
- Develop a data handling protocol that includes consent forms for outreach by 2026-02-20. 
- Train all outreach staff on GDPR compliance and data handling procedures by 2026-02-25.

feedback.title: Establish Volunteer Safety Protocols
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Create a comprehensive volunteer safety training program, including emergency procedures, by 2026-02-15. 
- Implement a buddy system for all outreach activities, ensuring at least 2 volunteers are paired for safety by 2026-02-20. 
- Develop a reporting procedure for any incidents or safety concerns, to be communicated to all volunteers by 2026-02-25.

feedback.title: Develop Surge Capacity Plan
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Create a surge capacity plan that outlines additional staffing and resource allocation during extreme heat events by 2026-03-01. 
- Identify at least 3 local organizations that can provide additional support during surges by 2026-03-05. 
- Conduct a risk assessment for potential surge scenarios and develop contingency plans by 2026-03-10.

feedback.title: Monitor Communication Channels
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must: 
- Establish a monitoring system for misinformation related to heatwave safety by 2026-02-20. 
- Develop a rapid response communication plan to counter misinformation, including key messages and channels, by 2026-02-25. 
- Train outreach staff on how to address misinformation during their interactions with residents by 2026-03-01.
